// Typed wrappers around window.electron.ipcRenderer.invoke.
// The renderer imports from here — never calls ipcRenderer directly.

import { IPC } from '@shared/ipc-channels'
import type {
  Task,
  Category,
  CategoryWithCount,
  Subtask,
  CreateTaskPayload,
  UpdateTaskPayload,
  CreateCategoryPayload,
  UpdateCategoryPayload,
  CreateSubtaskPayload,
  UpdateSubtaskPayload,
  PomodoroSession
} from '@shared/types'

const invoke = (channel: string, payload?: unknown) =>
  window.electron.ipcRenderer.invoke(channel, payload)

// ── Tasks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const getAllTasks = (opts?: {
  categoryId?: number
  includeComplete?: boolean
}): Promise<Task[]> => invoke(IPC.TASKS.GET_ALL, opts)

export const getTaskById = (id: number): Promise<Task | null> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.GET_BY_ID, { id })

export const createTask = (payload: CreateTaskPayload): Promise<Task> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.CREATE, payload)

export const updateTask = (payload: UpdateTaskPayload): Promise<Task> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.UPDATE, payload)

export const deleteTask = (id: number): Promise<void> => invoke(IPC.TASKS.DELETE, { id })

export const reorderTasks = (orderedIds: number[]): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.REORDER, { orderedIds })

export const completeTask = (id: number): Promise<Task> => invoke(IPC.TASKS.COMPLETE, { id })

// ── Categories ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const getAllCategories = (): Promise<CategoryWithCount[]> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.GET_ALL)

export const createCategory = (payload: CreateCategoryPayload): Promise<Category> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.CREATE, payload)

export const updateCategory = (payload: UpdateCategoryPayload): Promise<Category> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.UPDATE, payload)

export const deleteCategory = (id: number): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.DELETE, { id })

// ── Subtasks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const getSubtasksByTask = (taskId: number): Promise<Subtask[]> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.GET_BY_TASK, { taskId })

export const createSubtask = (payload: CreateSubtaskPayload): Promise<Subtask> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.CREATE, payload)

export const updateSubtask = (payload: UpdateSubtaskPayload): Promise<Subtask> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.UPDATE, payload)

export const deleteSubtask = (id: number): Promise<void> => invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.DELETE, { id })

export const reorderSubtasks = (taskId: number, orderedIds: number[]): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.REORDER, { taskId, orderedIds })

// ── Pomodoro ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const startPomodoroSession = (payload: {
  taskId?: number
  phase: PomodoroSession['phase']
  durationS: number
}): Promise<{ sessionId: number }> => invoke(IPC.POMODORO.START_SESSION, payload)

export const completePomodoroSession = (sessionId: number): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.POMODORO.COMPLETE_SESSION, { sessionId })

// ── App ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const minimizeToTray = (): Promise<void> => invoke(IPC.APP.MINIMIZE_TO_TRAY)
export const quitApp = (): Promise<void> => invoke(IPC.APP.QUIT)

import { create } from 'zustand'

interface AppState {
  // Which category is selected in the sidebar (null = "All Tasks")
  activeCategoryId: number | null
  setActiveCategoryId: (id: number | null) => void

  // Focus mode: hides everything except the current task + Pomodoro
  focusMode: boolean
  focusTaskId: number | null
  enterFocusMode: (taskId: number) => void
  exitFocusMode: () => void

  // Brain dump quick-add panel
  brainDumpOpen: boolean
  toggleBrainDump: () => void
  openBrainDump: () => void
  closeBrainDump: () => void
}

export const useAppStore = create<AppState>((set) => ({
  activeCategoryId: null,
  setActiveCategoryId: (id) => set({ activeCategoryId: id }),

  focusMode: false,
  focusTaskId: null,
  enterFocusMode: (taskId) => set({ focusMode: true, focusTaskId: taskId }),
  exitFocusMode: () => set({ focusMode: false, focusTaskId: null }),

  brainDumpOpen: false,
  toggleBrainDump: () => set((s) => ({ brainDumpOpen: !s.brainDumpOpen })),
  openBrainDump: () => set({ brainDumpOpen: true }),
  closeBrainDump: () => set({ brainDumpOpen: false })
}))

The app builds clean. Here's the summary of what was built and how to run it:

---

## The app is ready at `Documents/to-dos/adhd-todo/`

**To launch in dev mode (hot reload):**
```bash
cd adhd-todo
npm run dev
```

**To build a Windows installer:**
```bash
npm run pack
# outputs release/ADHD Todo Setup 1.0.0.exe
```

---

## What's in the app

| Feature | Details |
|---|---|
| **System tray** | Closing hides to tray; right-click → Open or Quit |
| **Dark mode** | Always dark, CSS variable-based theming |
| **Large clock** | HH:MM:SS with date, monospace to prevent layout shift |
| **DO BEFORE 5PM banner** | Auto-appears with amber countdown; turns red when overdue |
| **Categories sidebar** | Always visible, never collapses — task counts shown |
| **Tasks** | Color-coded left border, due dates, time estimates |
| **Subtasks** | Inline checklist, add with Enter |
| **Drag to reorder** | @dnd-kit with optimistic updates |
| **Recurring tasks** | Daily/weekly/monthly; next occurrence auto-created on complete |
| **Brain dump** | `Ctrl+Space` or button → type, Enter, stay open for rapid fire |
| **Focus mode** | Full-screen overlay, triggered per-task; `Esc` to exit |
| **Pomodoro timer** | 4×(25min work + 5min break) + 30min long break; SVG ring progress |
| **Gamification stub** | Empty placeholder; research agent findings incoming |
| **SQLite persistence** | Data survives restarts; stored in `%AppData%/adhd-todo/adhd-todo.db` |

The gamification research agent is still running in the background — when it finishes I'll save the findings to memory so we can implement them in a follow-up session.

I'll draw on my training knowledge — which includes extensive research, user forums, academic literature, and app design analysis through my August 2025 knowledge cutoff — to deliver the full research report. This is a topic I have substantial depth on.

---

# Gamification for ADHD Brains: Research Report

## Background: Why ADHD Changes the Gamification Calculus

ADHD involves a dysregulated dopamine system — not a dopamine deficiency, but impaired dopamine *signaling*. The brain struggles to anticipate future rewards, making long-horizon motivation nearly impossible. This creates specific design requirements:

- Rewards must be **immediate** or near-immediate (within seconds of completion)
- Feedback loops must be **frequent and variable** (variable ratio schedules are most compelling)
- Progress must be **visually concrete** — abstract points feel meaningless
- The system must be **forgiving of gaps** — shame is a known ADHD executive function killer
- Novelty is critical — ADHD brains habituate quickly, so the same reward loses power fast

---

## Part 1: Gamification Mechanics That Work for ADHD

### 1. Immediate Micro-Rewards (Highest Impact)
The dopamine hit must arrive the instant a task is completed — not after a daily summary. Visual and auditory micro-celebrations (particles, sounds, animations) trigger the reward pathway directly. The key insight from ADHD research: **the anticipation of reward is as important as the reward itself**. A satisfying "pop" animation on task completion creates a tiny dopamine event that makes the next task more approachable. Habitica uses this well; the health/XP bar updating immediately is more motivating than any badge.

### 2. XP + Leveling Systems (with Visible Progress)
A cumulative XP bar that **never goes backward** is critical. Levels provide identity ("I'm level 12") and give a sense of persistent progress even when tasks vary. The bar should be visible at all times, not hidden in a profile screen. The level-up moment is a high-dopamine event — make it theatrical.

### 3. Streaks — But Forgiving Ones
Streaks work as an intrinsic motivator but are the **single largest source of shame spirals** for ADHD users. Unforgiving streaks (Duolingo-style before they added streak freezes) cause users to abandon the app entirely the first time they break one. Best practice: implement "streak shields" or "grace periods" (Finch does this), or reframe streaks as "X out of last 7 days" rather than a consecutive-day counter. Habitica also sells "streak freeze" items.

### 4. Variable/Surprise Rewards
ADHD brains are especially sensitive to variable ratio schedules (the same mechanism as slot machines). Occasional surprise rewards — a random badge, a bonus XP event, a "lucky task" that awards 2x points — are more motivating than predictable fixed rewards. Forest uses a subtle version of this by randomizing tree types.

### 5. Progress Bars on Tasks Themselves
A progress bar on individual tasks (fed by subtask completion) gives moment-to-moment visual feedback. ADHD users often struggle to start tasks because they feel monolithic — seeing a bar at 40% complete is a powerful "re-entry" cue after distraction. This is a low-cost, high-impact feature.

### 6. Pomodoro Integration with Session Streaks
Pomodoro is one of the most ADHD-friendly productivity techniques because it time-boxes work and builds in scheduled breaks. Logging completed sessions and showing a "today's focus sessions" counter (not a multi-day streak — just today) is motivating without being shaming. Forest's entire model is built on this.

### 7. "Done" History / Evidence of Work
ADHD users frequently feel they've accomplished nothing even on productive days (time blindness). A persistent, visible log of completed tasks — especially one that shows *how much* was done today — combats this. Finch calls this a "self-care check-in" but the mechanism is the same: building evidence of effort. This is low-effort to implement and high emotional value.

---

## Part 2: What Successful Apps Have Done Well

### Habitica
- Turns tasks into RPG quests; completing tasks deals damage to enemies
- Party quests create social accountability (helpful for ADHD)
- Streak freeze items remove shame from occasional misses
- XP + gold for task completion immediately
- **Pitfall**: The RPG complexity can itself become a distraction/hyperfocus trap for ADHD users

### Forest
- Grows a virtual tree during a focus session; leaving the app kills it
- Visual, tangible metaphor for focus
- No shame mechanic — dead trees don't penalize streaks
- **Pitfall**: Novelty wears off quickly; once you've seen all trees, it loses power

### Finch (Self-Care Pet App)
- A virtual pet bird that you raise by completing self-care goals
- Grace periods built in — the bird doesn't die if you miss days
- Emotion-forward design reduces shame
- **Best ADHD insight**: Progress is framed as "caring for something else" — externalized motivation bypasses internal motivation deficits

### Focusmate
- Body doubling (working alongside a stranger on video call)
- Accountability through social presence, not points
- Highly effective for ADHD because it removes the "starting" barrier
- **Not directly applicable to a desktop to-do app** but worth noting

### Todoist (Karma System)
- Karma points for completing tasks, streaks for daily completions
- Productivity chart shows historical patterns
- **Pitfall**: Karma goes DOWN for overdue tasks — punitive mechanic that backfires for ADHD users who already have piles of overdue items

### Notion + TickTick
- TickTick has a simple habit tracker with rings (Apple Fitness-style) that ADHD users report enjoying
- The "ring" metaphor (fill the circle today) is more forgiving than a streak number

---

## Part 3: Anti-Patterns — What Backfires for ADHD Users

| Anti-Pattern | Why It Backfires |
|---|---|
| **Punitive streaks** (streak resets to 0) | One miss triggers shame → abandonment cascade |
| **Points that decrease** (overdue task penalties) | Reinforces negative self-image; ADHD users already struggle with guilt |
| **Complex onboarding to unlock gamification** | ADHD users won't complete multi-step setups; gamification must be on by default |
| **Notifications as the only reward trigger** | Notifications are often ignored/muted by ADHD users; reward must be in-app |
| **Long-horizon goals only** (annual challenges) | Dopamine system can't stay engaged over weeks; must have daily/hourly payoffs |
| **Leaderboards** | Comparing to others triggers shame if behind; ADHD users rarely "win" consistency contests |
| **All-or-nothing task credit** | If a task takes subtasks and only the final completion counts, partial effort goes unrewarded |
| **Hidden progress** | Points/XP buried in a profile page don't work — the reward must be immediately visible |

---

## Part 4: Concrete UI Features — What to Show, When, How Much Friction

### Task Completion Animation
- **What**: Particle burst or satisfying "pop" + checkmark animation when a task is marked complete
- **When**: Instant, on the completion click
- **Friction**: Zero — must be automatic, no settings to enable

### XP Bar (Always Visible)
- **What**: Persistent XP progress bar in the sidebar or header showing progress toward next level
- **When**: Visible at all times; animates on XP gain
- **Friction**: Zero — always on

### Today's Focus Counter
- **What**: "3 Pomodoros today" counter derived from `pomodoro_sessions`
- **When**: Visible in the header or sidebar, resets at midnight
- **Friction**: Zero — automatic from existing logging

### Task Progress Bar
- **What**: Visual progress bar on tasks that have subtasks (subtasks_complete / total_subtasks)
- **When**: Shows on any task that has subtasks; updates live as subtasks are checked
- **Friction**: Zero — automatic

### Streak with Shield
- **What**: "X-day streak" with a grace period of 1-2 days before resetting; display "shields" remaining
- **When**: Shown on the dashboard; celebrated with animation on streak increase
- **Friction**: Low — one optional setting for grace period length

### "What I Did Today" End-of-Day Panel
- **What**: Auto-generated list of completed tasks + pomodoros from today; can be dismissed
- **When**: Appears when user closes the app, or at a set time (e.g., 6 PM)
- **Friction**: Low — should be opt-in or easily dismissable

### Variable Bonus Events
- **What**: Occasional random "bonus XP" on a task (e.g., 1 in 10 tasks gets a "lucky" 2x XP marker)
- **When**: Assigned randomly at task creation or completion
- **Friction**: Zero — fully automatic

---

## Part 5: SQLite Integration

Your existing schema:

```
tasks          — id, title, completed, due_date, category_id, ...
subtasks       — id, task_id, title, completed, ...
categories     — id, name, color, ...
pomodoro_sessions — id, task_id, started_at, ended_at, duration, ...
```

### Feature-to-Table Mapping

| Feature | Source Tables | New Columns/Tables Needed |
|---|---|---|
| Task completion XP | `tasks` | Add `xp_value INT DEFAULT 10` to `tasks` |
| Subtask progress bar | `subtasks` | No new columns; computed at query time |
| Pomodoro "today" counter | `pomodoro_sessions` | No new columns; `WHERE DATE(started_at) = DATE('now')` |
| Streak tracking | `tasks` (completed date) | New table: `user_streaks` |
| XP / Leveling | New | New table: `user_stats` |
| Bonus XP multiplier | `tasks` | Add `xp_multiplier REAL DEFAULT 1.0` to `tasks` |
| "What I Did Today" | `tasks`, `pomodoro_sessions` | No new columns |
| Badges | New | New table: `badges` + `user_badges` |

### Recommended New Tables

```sql
-- Tracks cumulative user stats
CREATE TABLE user_stats (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    total_xp INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    level INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
    streak_days INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    streak_last_active DATE,
    streak_shields INTEGER DEFAULT 2,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Defines available badges
CREATE TABLE badges (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    icon TEXT,           -- emoji or icon identifier
    xp_reward INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    condition_type TEXT, -- e.g. 'tasks_completed', 'streak_days', 'pomodoros_today'
    condition_value INTEGER
);

-- Tracks which badges the user has earned
CREATE TABLE user_badges (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    badge_id INTEGER REFERENCES badges(id),
    earned_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

### New Columns on Existing Tables

```sql
-- tasks table additions
ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN xp_value INTEGER DEFAULT 10;
ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN xp_multiplier REAL DEFAULT 1.0;
ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN completed_at DATETIME;  -- if not already present; critical for streak logic

-- pomodoro_sessions table addition (if not already present)
ALTER TABLE pomodoro_sessions ADD COLUMN completed BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1;
-- (to distinguish abandoned sessions from completed ones)
```

---

## Part 6: Recommended Features — Priority Order

Priority is ordered by: dopamine impact per implementation effort, plus stability (won't cause shame spirals).

### Phase 1: Foundation (Build First — Highest Immediate Impact)

**1. Task Completion Animation**
- Pure frontend, zero DB changes needed
- Immediate dopamine payoff on every single task completion
- Implementation: CSS/React animation triggered on task `completed` state change

**2. XP System + Level Bar (Always Visible)**
- Requires `user_stats` table + `xp_value` on `tasks`
- The core loop that makes everything else meaningful
- Simple formula: tasks award 10–50 XP based on due-date pressure or manual priority; levels at 100 XP increments

**3. Pomodoro "Today" Counter**
- Zero DB changes (uses existing `pomodoro_sessions`)
- Single SQL query: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pomodoro_sessions WHERE DATE(started_at) = DATE('now') AND completed = 1`
- Visible in sidebar; deeply validating for ADHD users (combats time blindness)

### Phase 2: Retention (Build Second — Keeps Users Coming Back)

**4. Forgiving Streaks with Shields**
- Requires `streak_days`, `streak_last_active`, `streak_shields` in `user_stats`
- Daily cron/check on app open: if `streak_last_active` is yesterday → increment; if 2 days ago and shields > 0 → use shield; if older → reset
- Critical: frame as "protect your streak" not "you broke your streak"

**5. Subtask Progress Bars**
- Zero DB changes — computed from `subtasks` table
- `SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE completed = 1` vs total
- Renders on any task that has subtasks; shows percentage fill

**6. "What I Did Today" Completion Panel**
- Zero DB changes
- Triggered on app close or at configurable end-of-day time
- Lists task completions + pomodoro count for the day
- Opt-in or easily dismissable

### Phase 3: Delight (Build Third — Novelty and Surprise)

**7. Badges + Variable Bonus XP**
- Requires `badges`, `user_badges` tables + `xp_multiplier` on tasks
- Start with 10–15 badges covering: first task, 5-day streak, 10 pomodoros, night owl (completed after 10pm), early bird, etc.
- Variable bonus: randomly assign `xp_multiplier = 2.0` to ~10% of new tasks (surfaced as a small "★ bonus" label)

---

## Summary Table

| Feature | Effort | ADHD Impact | Build Order |
|---|---|---|---|
| Task completion animation | Low | Very High | 1 |
| XP + level bar | Medium | Very High | 2 |
| Pomodoro today counter | Low | High | 3 |
| Forgiving streaks | Medium | High | 4 |
| Subtask progress bars | Low | High | 5 |
| "What I did today" panel | Medium | High | 6 |
| Badges + variable XP | Medium-High | Medium | 7 |

---

## Key Design Principles to Carry Into Implementation

1. **Never subtract points or XP.** Only add. Punitive mechanics are incompatible with ADHD.
2. **All rewards must be immediate and visible.** Nothing buried in menus.
3. **Streaks must have grace periods.** One missed day should never reset a streak without a shield system.
4. **Progress must always be visible.** XP bar in the main layout, not a profile page.
5. **Celebrate effort, not just outcomes.** Pomodoro completion should award XP even if the associated task isn't finished.
6. **Keep it opt-outable.** Some ADHD users find gamification distracting — offer a "minimal mode."
7. **Novelty matters.** Plan to rotate badge sets or add new ones over time; the system will lose power if it feels "finished."

---

Agents used: none (all findings are from training knowledge — academic ADHD research, app design literature, user community reports, and UX studies through August 2025). WebFetch was requested but permission was denied.</result>

input.content:
---
name: ADHD Todo — Gamification Research Findings
description: Research findings on ADHD-effective gamification for the adhd-todo desktop app; includes prioritized feature list, anti-patterns, and SQLite schema additions
type: project
---

Gamification research completed for the adhd-todo Electron app (at `Documents/to-dos/adhd-todo/`).

**Why:** User has ADHD and wants dopamine-supportive gamification that avoids shame spirals.

**How to apply:** Use these findings to implement Phase 1–3 gamification in a follow-up session. Never use punitive mechanics (XP loss, unforgiving streak resets).

---

## Core ADHD Gamification Principles (must not violate these)
1. Never subtract points/XP — only add
2. All rewards must be immediate and visible (not buried in menus)
3. Streaks need grace periods / shields — one miss must never silently reset
4. Celebrate effort, not just outcomes (Pomodoro XP even if task isn't done)
5. Keep it opt-outable — some ADHD users find it distracting

---

## Recommended Features (Priority Order)

### Phase 1 — Foundation (Highest dopamine per effort)
1. **Task completion animation** — CSS particle burst on task complete; zero DB changes needed; pure renderer
2. **XP system + always-visible level bar** — `user_stats` table + `xp_value INT DEFAULT 10` on tasks; level bar in sidebar/header always shown
3. **Pomodoro "today" counter** — `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pomodoro_sessions WHERE DATE(started_at) = DATE('now') AND was_completed = 1`; already fully logged; combats ADHD time blindness

### Phase 2 — Retention
4. **Forgiving streaks with shields** — `streak_days`, `streak_last_active`, `streak_shields` on `user_stats`; check on app open; 2-day grace before reset; frame as "protect your streak"
5. **Subtask progress bars** — zero DB changes; computed from `subtasks` table; renders on any task with subtasks
6. **"What I Did Today" panel** — zero DB changes; appears on app close or configurable end-of-day; lists completions + pomodoro count; opt-in/dismissable

### Phase 3 — Delight
7. **Badges + variable bonus XP** — `badges` + `user_badges` tables; `xp_multiplier REAL DEFAULT 1.0` on tasks; randomly assign 2.0 to ~10% of new tasks; 10–15 starter badges

---

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- Punitive streaks (reset to 0 on first miss) → shame → app abandonment
- XP/points that decrease (Todoist Karma mistake)
- Leaderboards (ADHD users rarely "win" consistency contests)
- Long-horizon-only goals (weeks/months) — must have daily/hourly payoffs
- All-or-nothing task credit — subtask completion must award partial XP
- Hidden progress (profile pages) — must be visible in main layout

---

## Required DB Changes

### New tables:
```sql
CREATE TABLE user_stats (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    total_xp INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    level INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
    streak_days INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    streak_last_active DATE,
    streak_shields INTEGER DEFAULT 2,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE badges (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    icon TEXT,           -- emoji or icon identifier
    xp_reward INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    condition_type TEXT, -- 'tasks_completed' | 'streak_days' | 'pomodoros_today'
    condition_value INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE user_badges (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    badge_id INTEGER REFERENCES badges(id),
    earned_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

### Additions to existing tables:
```sql
ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN xp_value INTEGER DEFAULT 10;
ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN xp_multiplier REAL DEFAULT 1.0;
```

(`pomodoro_sessions` already has `was_completed` — no changes needed.)

---

## App Learning Notes (for this user)
- The Habitica RPG model works but can become a hyperfocus trap for ADHD
- Finch's "caring for something else" model bypasses internal motivation deficits — worth considering as a future theme
- Variable ratio rewards (slot machine mechanic) are especially compelling for ADHD dopamine systems
- Today-only counters (not multi-day streaks) are emotionally safer for Phase 1

---
name: ADHD Todo — App Architecture
description: Core architecture, tech stack, and file structure for the adhd-todo Electron desktop app built for a user with ADHD
type: project
---

Desktop to-do app built at `Documents/to-dos/adhd-todo/`. Fully implemented and building clean as of 2026-03-23.

**Why:** User has ADHD and needs a persistent, ADHD-friendly desktop to-do list.

**How to apply:** Use this context when continuing development on the adhd-todo app.

---

## Tech Stack
- Electron + electron-vite
- React 18 + TypeScript (renderer)
- Tailwind CSS (always dark, `class="dark"` on `<html>`)
- better-sqlite3 (SQLite at `%AppData%/adhd-todo/adhd-todo.db`)
- @dnd-kit (drag-to-reorder)
- date-fns (date math, used in both processes)
- Zustand (ephemeral UI state only)
- lucide-react (icons)

## Key Commands
```bash
cd Documents/to-dos/adhd-todo
npm run dev        # hot reload dev mode
npm run build      # production build
npm run rebuild    # re-run electron-rebuild for better-sqlite3
npm run pack       # build + Windows installer (.exe in release/)
```

## Features Implemented
- System tray (close hides to tray, double-click to reopen)
- Dark mode always-on
- Large live clock (HH:MM:SS) with date
- "DO BEFORE 5PM" amber countdown banner (auto-appears, turns red when overdue)
- Categories sidebar — always fully visible (ADHD: out of sight = forgotten)
- Tasks: title, category, color, due date, time estimate, subtasks, recurring pattern
- Drag-to-reorder with optimistic updates
- Color coding (per-task left border)
- Recurring tasks (daily/weekly/monthly — next occurrence auto-created on complete)
- Brain dump: Ctrl+Space opens quick-add strip; Enter adds, stays open, Escape closes
- Focus mode: full-screen overlay triggered per-task, Escape exits
- Pomodoro timer: 4×(25/5) + 30min long break; SVG ring progress; start/stop
- Pomodoro sessions logged to `pomodoro_sessions` table (ready for gamification)
- GamificationStub component (empty placeholder for Phase 1–3 gamification)

## IPC Pattern
- Shared types: `src/shared/types.ts`
- Channel constants: `src/shared/ipc-channels.ts` (IPC.TASKS.GET_ALL etc.)
- Main handlers: `src/main/ipc/*.ts`
- Renderer wrappers: `src/renderer/src/lib/ipc.ts` (typed async functions)
- Preload: `src/preload/index.ts` (contextBridge whitelist)

## Next Steps
1. Implement gamification Phase 1–3 (see gamification memory for details)
2. Icon upgrade — current icon is a flat purple 16x16 placeholder
3. Consider adding Windows startup-on-login option

Gamification findings saved to memory. Here's the key summary from the research agent:

---

## Gamification Research Results

**Phase 1 (build first — highest dopamine-per-effort):**
1. **Task completion animation** — particle burst the instant you check something off (zero DB changes, pure CSS/React)
2. **XP bar always visible** in the sidebar — never goes backward, levels up with a celebration
3. **"Pomodoro today" counter** — shows how many focus sessions you've done today (already fully logged, just needs a query)

**Phase 2 (retention):**
4. **Forgiving streaks with shields** — grace period before a streak resets, framed as "protect your streak" not "you failed"
5. **Subtask progress bars** — fills as you check off subtasks, gives re-entry cues after distraction
6. **"What I did today" end-of-day panel** — combats ADHD time blindness, shows evidence of your actual work

**Phase 3 (delight):**
7. **Badges + variable bonus XP** — random ~10% of tasks get a "lucky 2x XP" marker; slot-machine variable ratio is especially effective for ADHD dopamine systems

**The non-negotiable ADHD rules:** Never subtract XP, no unforgiving streak resets, no leaderboards, all rewards visible in the main UI (not buried in a profile page).

---

Whenever you're ready to run the app: `cd Documents/to-dos/adhd-todo && npm run dev`

Want to tackle gamification Phase 1 next, or is there anything in the core app to adjust first?

     3→/** Run once on startup. CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is idempotent. */
     4→export function runMigrations(db: Database.Database): void {

     6→  db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON')
     7→
     8→  db.exec(`
     9→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS categories (
    10→      id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    11→      name       TEXT    NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    12→      color      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '#6366f1',
    13→      sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    14→      created_at TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    15→    );
    16→
    17→    INSERT OR IGNORE INTO categories (name, color, sort_order)
    18→    VALUES ('Inbox', '#6366f1', 0);
    19→
    20→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (
    21→      id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    22→      category_id         INTEGER REFERENCES categories(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    23→      title               TEXT    NOT NULL,
    24→      color               TEXT,
    25→      due_date            TEXT,
    26→      due_before_5pm      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    27→      time_estimate_mins  INTEGER,
    28→      sort_order          INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    29→      is_complete         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    30→      completed_at        TEXT,
    31→      recurring_pattern   TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none',
    32→      recurring_base_id   INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    33→      created_at          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    34→      updated_at          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    35→    );
    36→
    37→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_category ON tasks(category_id);
    38→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_due_date  ON tasks(due_date);
    39→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_complete  ON tasks(is_complete);
    40→
    41→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS subtasks (
    42→      id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    43→      task_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    44→      title       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    45→      is_complete INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    46→      sort_order  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    47→      created_at  TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    48→    );
    49→

1→import type Database from 'better-sqlite3'
     2→import { addDays, addWeeks, addMonths, format } from 'date-fns'
     3→import type { Task, CreateTaskPayload, UpdateTaskPayload } from '@shared/types'
     4→
     5→function rowToTask(row: Record<string, unknown>): Task {
     6→  return {
     7→    id: row.id as number,
     8→    categoryId: row.category_id as number | null,
     9→    title: row.title as string,
    10→    color: row.color as string | null,
    11→    dueDate: row.due_date as string | null,
    12→    dueBefore5pm: Boolean(row.due_before_5pm),
    13→    timeEstimateMins: row.time_estimate_mins as number | null,
    14→    sortOrder: row.sort_order as number,
    15→    isComplete: Boolean(row.is_complete),
    16→    completedAt: row.completed_at as string | null,
    17→    recurringPattern: (row.recurring_pattern as Task['recurringPattern']) ?? 'none',
    18→    recurringBaseId: row.recurring_base_id as number | null,
    19→    createdAt: row.created_at as string,
    20→    updatedAt: row.updated_at as string
    21→  }
    22→}
    23→
    24→export function getAllTasks(
    25→  db: Database.Database,
    26→  opts: { categoryId?: number; includeComplete?: boolean } = {}
    27→): Task[] {
    28→  const conditions: string[] = []
    29→  const params: unknown[] = []
    30→
    31→  if (!opts.includeComplete) {
    32→    conditions.push('is_complete = 0')
    33→  }
    34→  if (opts.categoryId !== undefined) {
    35→    conditions.push('category_id = ?')
    36→    params.push(opts.categoryId)
    37→  }
    38→
    39→  const where = conditions.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}` : ''
    40→  const rows = db
    41→    .prepare(`SELECT * FROM tasks ${where} ORDER BY sort_order ASC, created_at ASC`)
    42→    .all(...params) as Record<string, unknown>[]
    43→
    44→  return rows.map(rowToTask)
    45→}
    46→
    47→export function getTaskById(db: Database.Database, id: number): Task | null {
    48→  const row = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE id = ?').get(id) as
    49→    | Record<string, unknown>
    50→    | undefined
    51→  return row ? rowToTask(row) : null
    52→}
    53→
    54→export function createTask(db: Database.Database, payload: CreateTaskPayload): Task {
    55→  const maxOrder = (
    56→    db.prepare('SELECT MAX(sort_order) AS m FROM tasks WHERE is_complete = 0').get() as {
    57→      m: number | null
    58→    }
    59→  ).m
    60→  const sortOrder = maxOrder != null ? maxOrder + 1 : 0
    61→
    62→  const result = db
    63→    .prepare(
    64→      `INSERT INTO tasks
    65→         (category_id, title, color, due_date, due_before_5pm, time_estimate_mins,
    66→          sort_order, is_complete, recurring_pattern, recurring_base_id)
    67→       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`
    68→    )
    69→    .run(
    70→      payload.categoryId ?? null,
    71→      payload.title,
    72→      payload.color ?? null,
    73→      payload.dueDate ?? null,
    74→      payload.dueBefore5pm ? 1 : 0,
    75→      payload.timeEstimateMins ?? null,
    76→      sortOrder,
    77→      payload.isComplete ? 1 : 0,
    78→      payload.recurringPattern ?? 'none',
    79→      payload.recurringBaseId ?? null
    80→    )
    81→
    82→  return getTaskById(db, result.lastInsertRowid as number)!
    83→}
    84→
    85→export function updateTask(db: Database.Database, payload: UpdateTaskPayload): Task {
    86→  const fields: string[] = []
    87→  const values: unknown[] = []
    88→
    89→  const map: Record<string, string> = {
    90→    categoryId: 'category_id',
    91→    title: 'title',
    92→    color: 'color',
    93→    dueDate: 'due_date',
    94→    dueBefore5pm: 'due_before_5pm',
    95→    timeEstimateMins: 'time_estimate_mins',
    96→    sortOrder: 'sort_order',
    97→    isComplete: 'is_complete',
    98→    completedAt: 'completed_at',
    99→    recurringPattern: 'recurring_pattern',
   100→    recurringBaseId: 'recurring_base_id'
   101→  }
   102→
   103→  for (const [key, col] of Object.entries(map)) {
   104→    if (key in payload && key !== 'id') {
   105→      fields.push(`${col} = ?`)
   106→      const val = payload[key as keyof UpdateTaskPayload]
   107→      // Convert booleans to 0/1 for SQLite
   108→      values.push(typeof val === 'boolean' ? (val ? 1 : 0) : val)
   109→    }
   110→  }
   111→
   112→  if (fields.length > 0) {
   113→    fields.push("updated_at = datetime('now')")
   114→    values.push(payload.id)
   115→    db.prepare(`UPDATE tasks SET ${fields.join(', ')} WHERE id = ?`).run(...values)
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  return getTaskById(db, payload.id)!
   119→}
   120→
   121→export function deleteTask(db: Database.Database, id: number): void {
   122→  db.prepare('DELETE FROM tasks WHERE id = ?').run(id)
   123→}
   124→
   125→/** Bulk-update sort_order based on an ordered array of IDs. */
   126→export function reorderTasks(db: Database.Database, orderedIds: number[]): void {
   127→  const stmt = db.prepare('UPDATE tasks SET sort_order = ? WHERE id = ?')
   128→  const bulk = db.transaction(() => {
   129→    orderedIds.forEach((id, index) => stmt.run(index, id))
   130→  })
   131→  bulk()
   132→}
   133→
   134→/**
   135→ * Mark a task complete. If it has a recurring pattern, generates the next occurrence.
   136→ * Returns the updated task.
   137→ */
   138→export function completeTask(db: Database.Database, id: number): Task {
   139→  const now = new Date().toISOString()
   140→  db.prepare(
   141→    `UPDATE tasks SET is_complete = 1, completed_at = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`
   142→  ).run(now, id)
   143→
   144→  const task = getTaskById(db, id)!
   145→
   146→  // Spawn next recurrence if applicable
   147→  if (task.recurringPattern !== 'none' && task.dueDate) {
   148→    const base = new Date(task.dueDate)
   149→    let nextDate: Date
   150→    if (task.recurringPattern === 'daily') nextDate = addDays(base, 1)
   151→    else if (task.recurringPattern === 'weekly') nextDate = addWeeks(base, 1)
   152→    else nextDate = addMonths(base, 1)
   153→
   154→    createTask(db, {
   155→      categoryId: task.categoryId,
   156→      title: task.title,
   157→      color: task.color,
   158→      dueDate: format(nextDate, 'yyyy-MM-dd'),
   159→      dueBefore5pm: task.dueBefore5pm,
   160→      timeEstimateMins: task.timeEstimateMins,
   161→      sortOrder: task.sortOrder,
   162→      isComplete: false,
   163→      recurringPattern: task.recurringPattern,
   164→      recurringBaseId: task.recurringBaseId ?? id
   165→    })
   166→  }
   167→
   168→  return task
   169→}
   170→

1→import { useState, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import type { Task, CreateTaskPayload, UpdateTaskPayload } from '@shared/types'
     3→import * as ipc from '@renderer/lib/ipc'
     4→import { arrayMove } from '@dnd-kit/sortable'
     5→
     6→export function useTasks(opts?: { categoryId?: number; includeComplete?: boolean }) {
     7→  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState<Task[]>([])
     8→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
     9→
    10→  const refresh = useCallback(async () => {
    11→    setLoading(true)
    12→    try {
    13→      setTasks(await ipc.getAllTasks(opts))
    14→    } finally {
    15→      setLoading(false)
    16→    }
    17→  }, [opts?.categoryId, opts?.includeComplete])
    18→
    19→  const addTask = useCallback(async (payload: CreateTaskPayload) => {
    20→    const newTask = await ipc.createTask(payload)
    21→    setTasks((prev) => [...prev, newTask])
    22→    return newTask
    23→  }, [])
    24→
    25→  const editTask = useCallback(async (payload: UpdateTaskPayload) => {
    26→    const updated = await ipc.updateTask(payload)
    27→    setTasks((prev) => prev.map((t) => (t.id === updated.id ? updated : t)))
    28→    return updated
    29→  }, [])
    30→
    31→  const removeTask = useCallback(async (id: number) => {
    32→    await ipc.deleteTask(id)
    33→    setTasks((prev) => prev.filter((t) => t.id !== id))
    34→  }, [])
    35→
    36→  /**
    37→   * Optimistically reorder tasks by dragging.
    38→   * oldIndex/newIndex are positions in the current `tasks` array.
    39→   */
    40→  const reorder = useCallback(
    41→    async (oldIndex: number, newIndex: number) => {
    42→      const reordered = arrayMove(tasks, oldIndex, newIndex)
    43→      setTasks(reordered) // optimistic update
    44→      try {
    45→        await ipc.reorderTasks(reordered.map((t) => t.id))
    46→      } catch {
    47→        // Revert on failure
    48→        setTasks(tasks)
    49→      }
    50→    },
    51→    [tasks]
    52→  )
    53→
    54→  const markComplete = useCallback(async (id: number) => {
    55→    await ipc.completeTask(id)
    56→    // Remove from list (complete tasks are hidden by default); caller can refresh if needed
    57→    setTasks((prev) => prev.filter((t) => t.id !== id))
    58→  }, [])
    59→
    60→  return { tasks, loading, refresh, addTask, editTask, removeTask, reorder, markComplete }
    61→}
    62→

input.content:
# ADHD Todo — Phase 2: Gamification + Launcher + Auto-start

## Context
Three additions to the existing Electron app at `Documents/to-dos/adhd-todo/`:
1. **Gamification Phase 1** — task completion animation, XP/level bar in sidebar, Pomodoro "today" counter
2. **Auto-start on login** — toggle in system tray menu using `app.setLoginItemSettings()`
3. **Clickable launcher** — `launch.vbs` script so the user can double-click to open the app without touching a terminal

---

## 1. Gamification Phase 1

### DB changes (`src/main/db/migrations.ts`)
Add to `runMigrations()` — safe to re-run (uses `IF NOT EXISTS` / `IF NOT COLUMN`):
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_stats (
  id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  total_xp   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  level      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  updated_at TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
INSERT OR IGNORE INTO user_stats (id, total_xp, level) VALUES (1, 0, 1);
ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN xp_value INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 10;
```
> SQLite doesn't support `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`. Wrap in a try/catch in JS so re-runs don't crash.

### New file: `src/main/db/gamification.ts`
- `getStats(db)` → `UserStats` — fetches row id=1 from `user_stats`
- `addXp(db, amount)` → `UserStats` — increments `total_xp`, recalculates `level = floor(total_xp / 100) + 1`, returns updated stats
- `getTodayPomodoroCount(db)` → `number` — `SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE DATE(started_at) = DATE('now') AND was_completed = 1 AND phase = 'work'`

### IPC (`src/shared/ipc-channels.ts`)
Add:
```ts
GAMIFICATION: {
  GET_STATS: 'gamification:get-stats',  // returns UserStats + todayPomodoros
}
```

### IPC handler (`src/main/ipc/gamification.ts` + register in `ipc/index.ts`)
- `gamification:get-stats` → `{ ...getStats(db), todayPomodoros: getTodayPomodoroCount(db) }`

### Hook XP into task completion (`src/main/ipc/tasks.ts`)
In the `IPC.TASKS.COMPLETE` handler: after calling `db.completeTask()`, call `gamification.addXp(db, task.xp_value)` and return `{ task, xpGained, stats }` instead of just `task`.
> Update `completeTask` in `db/tasks.ts` to also read `xp_value` from the row.

### Shared types (`src/shared/types.ts`)
Add:
```ts
export interface UserStats {
  totalXp: number
  level: number
  updatedAt: string
}
export interface CompleteTaskResult {
  task: Task
  xpGained: number
  stats: UserStats
  todayPomodoros: number
}
```
Add `xpValue: number` to `Task` interface.

### New hook: `src/renderer/src/hooks/useUserStats.ts`
- State: `{ totalXp, level, todayPomodoros, xpGained }`
- `refresh()` → calls `gamification:get-stats`
- `onTaskComplete(result: CompleteTaskResult)` → sets `xpGained` for animation, merges new stats, calls `refresh()` to update pomodoro count
- `xpGained` clears after 2s (used to show floating "+10 XP" label)

### New component: `src/renderer/src/components/gamification/XpBar.tsx`
Inserted in `Sidebar.tsx` between the header `<div>` and the `<nav>`:
```
[Level 3]  ████████░░░░  80/100 XP
🍅 4 today
```
- Level badge: `Lv. N` in primary color
- XP bar: Tailwind `bg-primary` fill, width = `(totalXp % 100)%`, `transition-all duration-700`
- Floating `+XP` toast: absolute-positioned, animates upward and fades (CSS keyframe), shown when `xpGained > 0`
- Pomodoro counter: tomato emoji + "N today"

### Completion animation (`src/renderer/src/components/tasks/TaskCard.tsx`)
- Add `completing` state (boolean)
- On checkbox click: set `completing = true` → wait 600ms → call `onComplete()`
- While `completing`: card gets `scale-95 opacity-50 bg-primary/10` transition classes
- Check icon briefly shows a filled `CheckSquare` in primary color before card disappears
- Add `animate-complete` keyframe to `globals.css`: flash green, scale up to 1.05, then scale down to 0.95 + fade

### Wire to App.tsx
- `useUserStats` called in `App.tsx`
- `handleCompleteTask` updated: receives `CompleteTaskResult`, passes `xpGained` / `stats` to `useUserStats.onTaskComplete()`
- `XpBar` receives stats as props from `App` → passed down to `Sidebar`

---

## 2. Auto-start on login (`src/main/tray.ts`)

Replace hardcoded `contextMenu` with a function `buildContextMenu(mainWindow)` that reads current state:
```ts
function buildContextMenu(mainWindow: BrowserWindow, tray: Tray): Menu {
  const openAtLogin = app.getLoginItemSettings().openAtLogin
  return Menu.buildFromTemplate([
    { label: 'Open', click: () => { mainWindow.show(); mainWindow.focus() } },
    { type: 'separator' },
    {
      label: 'Start on login',
      type: 'checkbox',
      checked: openAtLogin,
      click: () => {
        app.setLoginItemSettings({ openAtLogin: !openAtLogin })
        tray.setContextMenu(buildContextMenu(mainWindow, tray))
      }
    },
    { type: 'separator' },
    { label: 'Quit', click: () => app.quit() }
  ])
}
```
Call `tray.setContextMenu(buildContextMenu(mainWindow, tray))` on creation.

> Note: `setLoginItemSettings` works correctly in packaged builds. In `npm run dev` it registers `node.exe` — it won't do useful auto-start until the app is packaged with `npm run pack`.

---

## 3. Clickable Launcher (`launch.vbs` in project root)

A VBScript that opens the app silently (no black cmd window):
```vbs
Set WshShell = WScript.CreateObject("WScript.Shell")
WshShell.Run "cmd /c cd /d ""C:\Users\mbrow\Documents\to-dos\adhd-todo"" && npm run dev", 0, False
Set WshShell = Nothing
```
- `0` = hidden window mode
- `False` = don't wait for process to finish (async launch)
- User can right-click → "Create shortcut", then drag shortcut to desktop or pin to taskbar

---

## Files Changed Summary
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/main/db/migrations.ts` | Add `user_stats` table + `xp_value` column |
| `src/main/db/gamification.ts` | **NEW** — `getStats`, `addXp`, `getTodayPomodoroCount` |
| `src/main/db/tasks.ts` | `completeTask` returns `{ task, xpGained }` |
| `src/main/ipc/gamification.ts` | **NEW** — IPC handler |
| `src/main/ipc/tasks.ts` | Call `addXp` after complete; return richer result |
| `src/main/ipc/index.ts` | Register gamification handler |
| `src/main/tray.ts` | Add "Start on login" checkbox toggle |
| `src/shared/types.ts` | Add `UserStats`, `CompleteTaskResult`, `xpValue` on `Task` |
| `src/shared/ipc-channels.ts` | Add `GAMIFICATION` channels |
| `src/renderer/src/lib/ipc.ts` | Add `getStats()`, update `completeTask()` return type |
| `src/renderer/src/hooks/useUserStats.ts` | **NEW** — stats + xpGained state |
| `src/renderer/src/components/gamification/XpBar.tsx` | **NEW** — level, XP bar, pomodoro counter |
| `src/renderer/src/components/tasks/TaskCard.tsx` | Add `completing` state + animation |
| `src/renderer/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` | Add `XpBar` between header and nav |
| `src/renderer/src/globals.css` | Add `@keyframes task-complete` |
| `src/renderer/src/App.tsx` | Wire `useUserStats`, pass stats to Sidebar |
| `launch.vbs` | **NEW** — silent double-click launcher |

---

## Verification
1. `npm run dev` → app launches
2. Complete a task → card flashes, then fades out; XP bar in sidebar animates forward; "+10 XP" floats up
3. Complete a Pomodoro session → "N today" counter in sidebar increments
4. Tray right-click → "Start on login" checkbox toggles; run `npm run pack`, install, toggle → verify auto-start in Task Manager > Startup
5. Double-click `launch.vbs` → app opens, no cmd window visible

- `addXp(db, amount)` → `UserStats` — increments `total_xp`, recalculates `level = floor(total_xp / 100) + 1`, returns updated stats
- `getTodayPomodoroCount(db)` → `number` — `SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE DATE(started_at) = DATE('now') AND was_completed = 1 AND phase = 'work'`

### IPC (`src/shared/ipc-channels.ts`)
Add:
```ts
GAMIFICATION: {
  GET_STATS: 'gamification:get-stats',  // returns UserStats + todayPomodoros
}
```

### IPC handler (`src/main/ipc/gamification.ts` + register in `ipc/index.ts`)
- `gamification:get-stats` → `{ ...getStats(db), todayPomodoros: getTodayPomodoroCount(db) }`

### Hook XP into task completion (`src/main/ipc/tasks.ts`)
In the `IPC.TASKS.COMPLETE` handler: after calling `db.completeTask()`, call `gamification.addXp(db, task.xp_value)` and return `{ task, xpGained, stats }` instead of just `task`.
> Update `completeTask` in `db/tasks.ts` to also read `xp_value` from the row.

### Shared types (`src/shared/types.ts`)
Add:
```ts
export interface UserStats {
  totalXp: number
  level: number
  updatedAt: string
}
export interface CompleteTaskResult {
  task: Task
  xpGained: number
  stats: UserStats
  todayPomodoros: number
}
```
Add `xpValue: number` to `Task` interface.

### New hook: `src/renderer/src/hooks/useUserStats.ts`
- State: `{ totalXp, level, todayPomodoros, xpGained }`
- `refresh()` → calls `gamification:get-stats`
- `onTaskComplete(result: CompleteTaskResult)` → sets `xpGained` for animation, merges new stats, calls `refresh()` to update pomodoro count
- `xpGained` clears after 2s (used to show floating "+10 XP" label)

### New component: `src/renderer/src/components/gamification/XpBar.tsx`
Inserted in `Sidebar.tsx` between the header `<div>` and the `<nav>`:
```
[Level 3]  ████████░░░░  80/100 XP
🍅 4 today
```
- Level badge: `Lv. N` in primary color
- XP bar: Tailwind `bg-primary` fill, width = `(totalXp % 100)%`, `transition-all duration-700`
- Floating `+XP` toast: absolute-positioned, animates upward and fades (CSS keyframe), shown when `xpGained > 0`
- Pomodoro counter: tomato emoji + "N today"

### Completion animation (`src/renderer/src/components/tasks/TaskCard.tsx`)
- Add `completing` state (boolean)
- On checkbox click: set `completing = true` → wait 600ms → call `onComplete()`
- While `completing`: card gets `scale-95 opacity-50 bg-primary/10` transition classes
- Check icon briefly shows a filled `CheckSquare` in primary color before card disappears
- Add `animate-complete` keyframe to `globals.css`: flash green, scale up to 1.05, then scale down to 0.95 + fade

### Wire to App.tsx
- `useUserStats` called in `App.tsx`
- `handleCompleteTask` updated: receives `CompleteTaskResult`, passes `xpGained` / `stats` to `useUserStats.onTaskComplete()`
- `XpBar` receives stats as props from `App` → passed down to `Sidebar`

---

## 2. Auto-start on login (`src/main/tray.ts`)

Replace hardcoded `contextMenu` with a function `buildContextMenu(mainWindow)` that reads current state:
```ts
function buildContextMenu(mainWindow: BrowserWindow, tray: Tray): Menu {
  const openAtLogin = app.getLoginItemSettings().openAtLogin
  return Menu.buildFromTemplate([
    { label: 'Open', click: () => { mainWindow.show(); mainWindow.focus() } },
    { type: 'separator' },
    {
      label: 'Start on login',
      type: 'checkbox',
      checked: openAtLogin,
      click: () => {
        app.setLoginItemSettings({ openAtLogin: !openAtLogin })
        tray.setContextMenu(buildContextMenu(mainWindow, tray))
      }
    },
    { type: 'separator' },
    { label: 'Quit', click: () => app.quit() }
  ])
}
```
Call `tray.setContextMenu(buildContextMenu(mainWindow, tray))` on creation.

> Note: `setLoginItemSettings` works correctly in packaged builds. In `npm run dev` it registers `node.exe` — it won't do useful auto-start until the app is packaged with `npm run pack`.

---

## 3. Clickable Launcher (`launch.vbs` in project root)

A VBScript that opens the app silently (no black cmd window):
```vbs
Set WshShell = WScript.CreateObject("WScript.Shell")
WshShell.Run "cmd /c cd /d ""C:\Users\mbrow\Documents\to-dos\adhd-todo"" && npm run dev", 0, False
Set WshShell = Nothing
```
- `0` = hidden window mode
- `False` = don't wait for process to finish (async launch)
- User can right-click → "Create shortcut", then drag shortcut to desktop or pin to taskbar

---

## Files Changed Summary
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/main/db/migrations.ts` | Add `user_stats` table + `xp_value` column |
| `src/main/db/gamification.ts` | **NEW** — `getStats`, `addXp`, `getTodayPomodoroCount` |
| `src/main/db/tasks.ts` | `completeTask` returns `{ task, xpGained }` |
| `src/main/ipc/gamification.ts` | **NEW** — IPC handler |
| `src/main/ipc/tasks.ts` | Call `addXp` after complete; return richer result |
| `src/main/ipc/index.ts` | Register gamification handler |
| `src/main/tray.ts` | Add "Start on login" checkbox toggle |
| `src/shared/types.ts` | Add `UserStats`, `CompleteTaskResult`, `xpValue` on `Task` |
| `src/shared/ipc-channels.ts` | Add `GAMIFICATION` channels |
| `src/renderer/src/lib/ipc.ts` | Add `getStats()`, update `completeTask()` return type |
| `src/renderer/src/hooks/useUserStats.ts` | **NEW** — stats + xpGained state |
| `src/renderer/src/components/gamification/XpBar.tsx` | **NEW** — level, XP bar, pomodoro counter |
| `src/renderer/src/components/tasks/TaskCard.tsx` | Add `completing` state + animation |
| `src/renderer/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` | Add `XpBar` between header and nav |
| `src/renderer/src/globals.css` | Add `@keyframes task-complete` |
| `src/renderer/src/App.tsx` | Wire `useUserStats`, pass stats to Sidebar |
| `launch.vbs` | **NEW** — silent double-click launcher |

---

## Verification
1. `npm run dev` → app launches
2. Complete a task → card flashes, then fades out; XP bar in sidebar animates forward; "+10 XP" floats up
3. Complete a Pomodoro session → "N today" counter in sidebar increments
4. Tray right-click → "Start on login" checkbox toggles; run `npm run pack`, install, toggle → verify auto-start in Task Manager > Startup
5. Double-click `launch.vbs` → app opens, no cmd window visible

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ADHD To-Do Desktop App — Implementation Plan

## Context
Build a brand-new Electron desktop to-do app for a user with ADHD. The app must be dark-mode only, persist data via SQLite, live in the system tray, and include ADHD-friendly features: large live clock, "DO BEFORE 5PM" countdown, focus mode, Pomodoro timer (4×25/5 + 30min break), brain dump quick-add, subtasks with time estimates, drag-to-reorder, color coding, and recurring tasks. A gamification research agent is running in the background; its findings will be implemented in a follow-up session.

## Tech Stack
- **Electron** + **electron-vite** (scaffolded via `npm create @quick-start/electron`)
- **React 18** + **TypeScript** (renderer)
- **Tailwind CSS** + **shadcn/ui** (dark UI components)
- **better-sqlite3** (SQLite; native addon, requires `electron-rebuild`)
- **@dnd-kit** (drag-and-drop)
- **date-fns** (date math in both processes)
- **Zustand** (ephemeral UI state only — not persisted task data)

## File Structure
```
adhd-todo/
├── electron.vite.config.ts
├── package.json / tsconfig*.json / tailwind.config.ts
├── resources/icon.ico
└── src/
    ├── main/
    │   ├── index.ts          # BrowserWindow, tray, app lifecycle
    │   ├── tray.ts           # SystemTray + context menu
    │   ├── window.ts         # Window helpers
    │   ├── ipc/
    │   │   ├── index.ts      # Registers all handlers
    │   │   ├── tasks.ts      # CRUD + reorder + complete (handles recurrence)
    │   │   ├── categories.ts
    │   │   ├── subtasks.ts
    │   │   └── pomodoro.ts   # Session start/complete logging
    │   └── db/
    │       ├── index.ts      # Open DB + run migrations
    │       ├── migrations.ts # All CREATE TABLE statements
    │       ├── tasks.ts / categories.ts / subtasks.ts / pomodoro.ts
    ├── preload/
    │   └── index.ts          # contextBridge whitelist of ipcRenderer.invoke
    ├── renderer/
    │   ├── main.tsx / App.tsx / globals.css
    │   ├── components/
    │   │   ├── layout/       AppShell, Header, Sidebar
    │   │   ├── clock/        LiveClock
    │   │   ├── banner/       Before5pmBanner
    │   │   ├── tasks/        TaskList, TaskCard, TaskForm, SubtaskList, RecurringBadge
    │   │   ├── categories/   CategoryItem, CategoryForm
    │   │   ├── braindump/    BrainDumpInput (FAB + Ctrl+Space shortcut)
    │   │   ├── focus/        FocusMode, PomodoroTimer
    │   │   └── gamification/ GamificationStub (empty placeholder)
    │   ├── hooks/            useTasks, useCategories, useSubtasks, useLiveClock,
    │   │                     useBefore5pm, usePomodoro, useDragOrder
    │   ├── store/            appStore.ts (Zustand: activeCategory, focusMode,
    │   │                     focusTaskId, brainDumpOpen)
    │   └── lib/              ipc.ts (typed wrappers), utils.ts
    └── shared/
        ├── types.ts          # Task, Category, Subtask, PomodoroSession interfaces
        └── ipc-channels.ts   # Channel name constants
```

## SQLite Schema (src/main/db/migrations.ts)
```sql
PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS categories (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  color TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '#6366f1',
  sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
INSERT OR IGNORE INTO categories (name, color) VALUES ('Inbox', '#6366f1');

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  category_id INTEGER REFERENCES categories(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  color TEXT,
  due_date TEXT,
  due_before_5pm INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  time_estimate_mins INTEGER,
  sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  is_complete INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  completed_at TEXT,
  recurring_pattern TEXT,       -- 'none'|'daily'|'weekly'|'monthly'
  recurring_base_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS subtasks (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  task_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_complete INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pomodoro_sessions (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  task_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  phase TEXT NOT NULL,          -- 'work'|'short_break'|'long_break'
  duration_s INTEGER NOT NULL,
  started_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
  completed_at TEXT,
  was_completed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);
```

## IPC Channels (src/shared/ipc-channels.ts)
- `tasks:get-all`, `tasks:get-by-id`, `tasks:create`, `tasks:update`, `tasks:delete`, `tasks:reorder`, `tasks:complete`
- `categories:get-all` (returns `CategoryWithCount`), `categories:create`, `categories:update`, `categories:delete`
- `subtasks:get-by-task`, `subtasks:create`, `subtasks:update`, `subtasks:delete`, `subtasks:reorder`
- `pomodoro:start-session`, `pomodoro:complete-session`
- `app:minimize-to-tray`, `app:quit`

## Key Implementation Notes

### Critical build notes
- `better-sqlite3` is a native addon — run `npx electron-rebuild -f -w better-sqlite3` after install
- Mark `better-sqlite3` as `external` in the main-process Vite config
- Packaging: add `"asarUnpack": ["**/*.node"]` to electron-builder config

### System Tray
- `mainWindow.on('close', e => { e.preventDefault(); mainWindow.hide() })` — hides instead of closing
- `app.on('before-quit')` sets a flag to allow real exit from tray menu "Quit"

### Dark Mode
- Tailwind `darkMode: 'class'`; add `class="dark"` to `<html>` unconditionally in `index.html`

### Clock (`useLiveClock` + `LiveClock.tsx`)
- `setInterval` every 1s updating a `Date` state; cleanup in `useEffect` return
- Display: `text-7xl font-mono` to prevent layout shift; dimmed seconds

### Before 5PM Banner (`useBefore5pm` + `Before5pmBanner.tsx`)
- Filter tasks: `due_before_5pm=true AND is_complete=false`
- Countdown = `differenceInSeconds(setHours(today,17,0,0,0), now)`; show "OVERDUE" in red after 5pm
- Style: full-width amber banner with `animate-pulse` border

### Categories Sidebar — "always visible" ADHD fix
- Fixed sidebar, never collapses, always renders all categories
- `categories:get-all` returns task count per category via JOIN
- "All Tasks" is a hardcoded first entry (no DB row)
- Active category stored in Zustand; filters `tasks:get-all` payload

### Brain Dump (`BrainDumpInput.tsx`)
- FAB fixed bottom-right; `Ctrl+Space` global keydown listener toggles it
- On Enter: `tasks:create` with title + active category (or Inbox). Stay open for rapid entry. Escape closes.

### Focus Mode (`FocusMode.tsx`)
- Full-screen `position: fixed inset-0 z-50` overlay; toggled via Zustand `focusMode`
- TaskCard "Focus" button sets `focusTaskId` in store before entering
- `PomodoroTimer` only renders inside FocusMode

### Pomodoro (`usePomodoro.ts`)
- State machine: `idle → work(25m) → short_break(5m)` × 4, then `long_break(30m)`, repeat
- `setInterval` countdown; `sessionIndex` 0–3 tracks position in cycle
- SVG circle arc (`stroke-dashoffset`) for progress; 4 dot indicators
- IPC only at session start/end (for logging); timer logic stays in renderer

### Drag-to-Reorder (`@dnd-kit`)
- `DndContext` + `SortableContext` wrapping `TaskList`; each `TaskCard` uses `useSortable`
- `onDragEnd`: `arrayMove` → optimistic local update → `tasks:reorder` IPC
- Revert on IPC failure

### Recurring Tasks
- On `tasks:complete` for a recurring task: mark complete, INSERT new row with `due_date` advanced by `addDays`/`addWeeks`/`addMonths` (date-fns in main process)

### Gamification
- `GamificationStub.tsx` — empty component with a TODO comment
- `pomodoro_sessions` table already logs data for future use
- Research agent running in background; findings saved to memory for next session

## Build & Package Commands
```bash
npm create @quick-start/electron adhd-todo -- --template react-ts
cd adhd-todo
npm install better-sqlite3 @dnd-kit/core @dnd-kit/sortable @dnd-kit/utilities date-fns zustand
npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer electron-rebuild electron-builder
npx tailwindcss init -p
npx shadcn@latest init
npx shadcn@latest add button dialog input label badge tooltip
npx electron-rebuild -f -w better-sqlite3
npm run dev          # hot reload
npm run build && npx electron-builder --win   # produces .exe in release/
```

## Verification
1. `npm run dev` — app launches, tray icon appears, window opens/hides correctly
2. Add a task → appears in list; reload app → task persists (SQLite)
3. Mark a recurring daily task complete → new task appears with tomorrow's date
4. Brain dump: `Ctrl+Space` → type → Enter → task appears instantly
5. Pomodoro: start → 25min countdown → auto-transitions to break → 4th break is 30min
6. Before5pmBanner: add a task with `due_before_5pm=true` → banner appears with countdown
7. Drag a task → order persists after reload

## Gamification (follow-up)
- Background agent `ac5292acbafe192e8` is researching ADHD gamification patterns
- Findings will be saved to memory; implement in a separate session after core app is complete

# ADHD Todo — Phase 2: Gamification + Launcher + Auto-start

## Context
Three additions to the existing Electron app at `Documents/to-dos/adhd-todo/`:
1. **Gamification Phase 1** — task completion animation, XP/level bar in sidebar, Pomodoro "today" counter
2. **Auto-start on login** — toggle in system tray menu using `app.setLoginItemSettings()`
3. **Clickable launcher** — `launch.vbs` script so the user can double-click to open the app without touching a terminal

---

## 1. Gamification Phase 1

### DB changes (`src/main/db/migrations.ts`)
Add to `runMigrations()` — safe to re-run (uses `IF NOT EXISTS` / `IF NOT COLUMN`):
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_stats (
  id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  total_xp   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  level      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  updated_at TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
INSERT OR IGNORE INTO user_stats (id, total_xp, level) VALUES (1, 0, 1);
ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN xp_value INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 10;
```
> SQLite doesn't support `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`. Wrap in a try/catch in JS so re-runs don't crash.

### New file: `src/main/db/gamification.ts`
- `getStats(db)` → `UserStats` — fetches row id=1 from `user_stats`
- `addXp(db, amount)` → `UserStats` — increments `total_xp`, recalculates `level = floor(total_xp / 100) + 1`, returns updated stats
- `getTodayPomodoroCount(db)` → `number` — `SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE DATE(started_at) = DATE('now') AND was_completed = 1 AND phase = 'work'`

### IPC (`src/shared/ipc-channels.ts`)
Add:
```ts
GAMIFICATION: {
  GET_STATS: 'gamification:get-stats',  // returns UserStats + todayPomodoros
}
```

### IPC handler (`src/main/ipc/gamification.ts` + register in `ipc/index.ts`)
- `gamification:get-stats` → `{ ...getStats(db), todayPomodoros: getTodayPomodoroCount(db) }`

### Hook XP into task completion (`src/main/ipc/tasks.ts`)
In the `IPC.TASKS.COMPLETE` handler: after calling `db.completeTask()`, call `gamification.addXp(db, task.xp_value)` and return `{ task, xpGained, stats }` instead of just `task`.
> Update `completeTask` in `db/tasks.ts` to also read `xp_value` from the row.

### Shared types (`src/shared/types.ts`)
Add:
```ts
export interface UserStats {
  totalXp: number
  level: number
  updatedAt: string
}
export interface CompleteTaskResult {
  task: Task
  xpGained: number
  stats: UserStats
  todayPomodoros: number
}
```
Add `xpValue: number` to `Task` interface.

### New hook: `src/renderer/src/hooks/useUserStats.ts`
- State: `{ totalXp, level, todayPomodoros, xpGained }`
- `refresh()` → calls `gamification:get-stats`
- `onTaskComplete(result: CompleteTaskResult)` → sets `xpGained` for animation, merges new stats, calls `refresh()` to update pomodoro count
- `xpGained` clears after 2s (used to show floating "+10 XP" label)

### New component: `src/renderer/src/components/gamification/XpBar.tsx`
Inserted in `Sidebar.tsx` between the header `<div>` and the `<nav>`:
```
[Level 3]  ████████░░░░  80/100 XP
🍅 4 today
```
- Level badge: `Lv. N` in primary color
- XP bar: Tailwind `bg-primary` fill, width = `(totalXp % 100)%`, `transition-all duration-700`
- Floating `+XP` toast: absolute-positioned, animates upward and fades (CSS keyframe), shown when `xpGained > 0`
- Pomodoro counter: tomato emoji + "N today"

### Completion animation (`src/renderer/src/components/tasks/TaskCard.tsx`)
- Add `completing` state (boolean)
- On checkbox click: set `completing = true` → wait 600ms → call `onComplete()`
- While `completing`: card gets `scale-95 opacity-50 bg-primary/10` transition classes
- Check icon briefly shows a filled `CheckSquare` in primary color before card disappears
- Add `animate-complete` keyframe to `globals.css`: flash green, scale up to 1.05, then scale down to 0.95 + fade

### Wire to App.tsx
- `useUserStats` called in `App.tsx`
- `handleCompleteTask` updated: receives `CompleteTaskResult`, passes `xpGained` / `stats` to `useUserStats.onTaskComplete()`
- `XpBar` receives stats as props from `App` → passed down to `Sidebar`

---

## 2. Auto-start on login (`src/main/tray.ts`)

Replace hardcoded `contextMenu` with a function `buildContextMenu(mainWindow)` that reads current state:
```ts
function buildContextMenu(mainWindow: BrowserWindow, tray: Tray): Menu {
  const openAtLogin = app.getLoginItemSettings().openAtLogin
  return Menu.buildFromTemplate([
    { label: 'Open', click: () => { mainWindow.show(); mainWindow.focus() } },
    { type: 'separator' },
    {
      label: 'Start on login',
      type: 'checkbox',
      checked: openAtLogin,
      click: () => {
        app.setLoginItemSettings({ openAtLogin: !openAtLogin })
        tray.setContextMenu(buildContextMenu(mainWindow, tray))
      }
    },
    { type: 'separator' },
    { label: 'Quit', click: () => app.quit() }
  ])
}
```
Call `tray.setContextMenu(buildContextMenu(mainWindow, tray))` on creation.

> Note: `setLoginItemSettings` works correctly in packaged builds. In `npm run dev` it registers `node.exe` — it won't do useful auto-start until the app is packaged with `npm run pack`.

---

## 3. Clickable Launcher (`launch.vbs` in project root)

A VBScript that opens the app silently (no black cmd window):
```vbs
Set WshShell = WScript.CreateObject("WScript.Shell")
WshShell.Run "cmd /c cd /d ""C:\Users\mbrow\Documents\to-dos\adhd-todo"" && npm run dev", 0, False
Set WshShell = Nothing
```
- `0` = hidden window mode
- `False` = don't wait for process to finish (async launch)
- User can right-click → "Create shortcut", then drag shortcut to desktop or pin to taskbar

---

## Files Changed Summary
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/main/db/migrations.ts` | Add `user_stats` table + `xp_value` column |
| `src/main/db/gamification.ts` | **NEW** — `getStats`, `addXp`, `getTodayPomodoroCount` |
| `src/main/db/tasks.ts` | `completeTask` returns `{ task, xpGained }` |
| `src/main/ipc/gamification.ts` | **NEW** — IPC handler |
| `src/main/ipc/tasks.ts` | Call `addXp` after complete; return richer result |
| `src/main/ipc/index.ts` | Register gamification handler |
| `src/main/tray.ts` | Add "Start on login" checkbox toggle |
| `src/shared/types.ts` | Add `UserStats`, `CompleteTaskResult`, `xpValue` on `Task` |
| `src/shared/ipc-channels.ts` | Add `GAMIFICATION` channels |
| `src/renderer/src/lib/ipc.ts` | Add `getStats()`, update `completeTask()` return type |
| `src/renderer/src/hooks/useUserStats.ts` | **NEW** — stats + xpGained state |
| `src/renderer/src/components/gamification/XpBar.tsx` | **NEW** — level, XP bar, pomodoro counter |
| `src/renderer/src/components/tasks/TaskCard.tsx` | Add `completing` state + animation |
| `src/renderer/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` | Add `XpBar` between header and nav |
| `src/renderer/src/globals.css` | Add `@keyframes task-complete` |
| `src/renderer/src/App.tsx` | Wire `useUserStats`, pass stats to Sidebar |
| `launch.vbs` | **NEW** — silent double-click launcher |

---

## Verification
1. `npm run dev` → app launches
2. Complete a task → card flashes, then fades out; XP bar in sidebar animates forward; "+10 XP" floats up
3. Complete a Pomodoro session → "N today" counter in sidebar increments
4. Tray right-click → "Start on login" checkbox toggles; run `npm run pack`, install, toggle → verify auto-start in Task Manager > Startup
5. Double-click `launch.vbs` → app opens, no cmd window visible

content.content:
1→import type Database from 'better-sqlite3'
     2→
     3→/** Run once on startup. CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is idempotent. */
     4→export function runMigrations(db: Database.Database): void {
     5→  db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL')
     6→  db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON')
     7→
     8→  db.exec(`
     9→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS categories (
    10→      id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    11→      name       TEXT    NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    12→      color      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '#6366f1',
    13→      sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    14→      created_at TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    15→    );
    16→
    17→    INSERT OR IGNORE INTO categories (name, color, sort_order)
    18→    VALUES ('Inbox', '#6366f1', 0);
    19→
    20→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (
    21→      id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    22→      category_id         INTEGER REFERENCES categories(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    23→      title               TEXT    NOT NULL,
    24→      color               TEXT,
    25→      due_date            TEXT,
    26→      due_before_5pm      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    27→      time_estimate_mins  INTEGER,
    28→      sort_order          INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    29→      is_complete         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    30→      completed_at        TEXT,
    31→      recurring_pattern   TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none',
    32→      recurring_base_id   INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    33→      created_at          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    34→      updated_at          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    35→    );
    36→
    37→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_category ON tasks(category_id);
    38→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_due_date  ON tasks(due_date);
    39→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_complete  ON tasks(is_complete);
    40→

content.text: Now update the DB tasks.ts to return xpValue, and the IPC tasks handler:

content.content:
1→import type Database from 'better-sqlite3'
     2→import { addDays, addWeeks, addMonths, format } from 'date-fns'
     3→import type { Task, CreateTaskPayload, UpdateTaskPayload } from '@shared/types'
     4→
     5→function rowToTask(row: Record<string, unknown>): Task {
     6→  return {
     7→    id: row.id as number,
     8→    categoryId: row.category_id as number | null,
     9→    title: row.title as string,
    10→    color: row.color as string | null,
    11→    dueDate: row.due_date as string | null,
    12→    dueBefore5pm: Boolean(row.due_before_5pm),
    13→    timeEstimateMins: row.time_estimate_mins as number | null,
    14→    sortOrder: row.sort_order as number,
    15→    isComplete: Boolean(row.is_complete),
    16→    completedAt: row.completed_at as string | null,
    17→    recurringPattern: (row.recurring_pattern as Task['recurringPattern']) ?? 'none',
    18→    recurringBaseId: row.recurring_base_id as number | null,
    19→    createdAt: row.created_at as string,
    20→    updatedAt: row.updated_at as string
    21→  }
    22→}
    23→
    24→export function getAllTasks(
    25→  db: Database.Database,
    26→  opts: { categoryId?: number; includeComplete?: boolean } = {}
    27→): Task[] {
    28→  const conditions: string[] = []
    29→  const params: unknown[] = []
    30→
    31→  if (!opts.includeComplete) {
    32→    conditions.push('is_complete = 0')
    33→  }
    34→  if (opts.categoryId !== undefined) {
    35→    conditions.push('category_id = ?')
    36→    params.push(opts.categoryId)
    37→  }
    38→
    39→  const where = conditions.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}` : ''
    40→  const rows = db
    41→    .prepare(`SELECT * FROM tasks ${where} ORDER BY sort_order ASC, created_at ASC`)
    42→    .all(...params) as Record<string, unknown>[]
    43→
    44→  return rows.map(rowToTask)
    45→}
    46→
    47→export function getTaskById(db: Database.Database, id: number): Task | null {
    48→  const row = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE id = ?').get(id) as
    49→    | Record<string, unknown>
    50→    | undefined
    51→  return row ? rowToTask(row) : null
    52→}
    53→
    54→export function createTask(db: Database.Database, payload: CreateTaskPayload): Task {
    55→  const maxOrder = (
    56→    db.prepare('SELECT MAX(sort_order) AS m FROM tasks WHERE is_complete = 0').get() as {
    57→      m: number | null
    58→    }
    59→  ).m
    60→  const sortOrder = maxOrder != null ? maxOrder + 1 : 0
    61→
    62→  const result = db
    63→    .prepare(
    64→      `INSERT INTO tasks
    65→         (category_id, title, color, due_date, due_before_5pm, time_estimate_mins,
    66→          sort_order, is_complete, recurring_pattern, recurring_base_id)
    67→       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`
    68→    )
    69→    .run(
    70→      payload.categoryId ?? null,
    71→      payload.title,
    72→      payload.color ?? null,
    73→      payload.dueDate ?? null,
    74→      payload.dueBefore5pm ? 1 : 0,
    75→      payload.timeEstimateMins ?? null,
    76→      sortOrder,
    77→      payload.isComplete ? 1 : 0,
    78→      payload.recurringPattern ?? 'none',
    79→      payload.recurringBaseId ?? null
    80→    )
    81→
    82→  return getTaskById(db, result.lastInsertRowid as number)!
    83→}
    84→
    85→export function updateTask(db: Database.Database, payload: UpdateTaskPayload): Task {
    86→  const fields: string[] = []
    87→  const values: unknown[] = []
    88→
    89→  const map: Record<string, string> = {
    90→    categoryId: 'category_id',
    91→    title: 'title',
    92→    color: 'color',
    93→    dueDate: 'due_date',
    94→    dueBefore5pm: 'due_before_5pm',
    95→    timeEstimateMins: 'time_estimate_mins',
    96→    sortOrder: 'sort_order',
    97→    isComplete: 'is_complete',
    98→    completedAt: 'completed_at',
    99→    recurringPattern: 'recurring_pattern',
   100→    recurringBaseId: 'recurring_base_id'
   101→  }
   102→
   103→  for (const [key, col] of Object.entries(map)) {
   104→    if (key in payload && key !== 'id') {
   105→      fields.push(`${col} = ?`)
   106→      const val = payload[key as keyof UpdateTaskPayload]
   107→      // Convert booleans to 0/1 for SQLite
   108→      values.push(typeof val === 'boolean' ? (val ? 1 : 0) : val)
   109→    }
   110→  }
   111→
   112→  if (fields.length > 0) {
   113→    fields.push("updated_at = datetime('now')")
   114→    values.push(payload.id)
   115→    db.prepare(`UPDATE tasks SET ${fields.join(', ')} WHERE id = ?`).run(...values)
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  return getTaskById(db, payload.id)!
   119→}
   120→
   121→export function deleteTask(db: Database.Database, id: number): void {
   122→  db.prepare('DELETE FROM tasks WHERE id = ?').run(id)
   123→}
   124→
   125→/** Bulk-update sort_order based on an ordered array of IDs. */
   126→export function reorderTasks(db: Database.Database, orderedIds: number[]): void {
   127→  const stmt = db.prepare('UPDATE tasks SET sort_order = ? WHERE id = ?')
   128→  const bulk = db.transaction(() => {
   129→    orderedIds.forEach((id, index) => stmt.run(index, id))
   130→  })
   131→  bulk()
   132→}
   133→
   134→/**
   135→ * Mark a task complete. If it has a recurring pattern, generates the next occurrence.
   136→ * Returns the updated task.
   137→ */
   138→export function completeTask(db: Database.Database, id: number): Task {
   139→  const now = new Date().toISOString()
   140→  db.prepare(
   141→    `UPDATE tasks SET is_complete = 1, completed_at = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`
   142→  ).run(now, id)
   143→
   144→  const task = getTaskById(db, id)!
   145→
   146→  // Spawn next recurrence if applicable
   147→  if (task.recurringPattern !== 'none' && task.dueDate) {
   148→    const base = new Date(task.dueDate)
   149→    let nextDate: Date
   150→    if (task.recurringPattern === 'daily') nextDate = addDays(base, 1)
   151→    else if (task.recurringPattern === 'weekly') nextDate = addWeeks(base, 1)
   152→    else nextDate = addMonths(base, 1)
   153→
   154→    createTask(db, {
   155→      categoryId: task.categoryId,
   156→      title: task.title,
   157→      color: task.color,
   158→      dueDate: format(nextDate, 'yyyy-MM-dd'),
   159→      dueBefore5pm: task.dueBefore5pm,
   160→      timeEstimateMins: task.timeEstimateMins,
   161→      sortOrder: task.sortOrder,
   162→      isComplete: false,
   163→      recurringPattern: task.recurringPattern,
   164→      recurringBaseId: task.recurringBaseId ?? id
   165→    })
   166→  }
   167→
   168→  return task
   169→}
   170→
