import { ipcMain } from 'electron'
import { IPC } from '@shared/ipc-channels'
import type { CreateTaskPayload, UpdateTaskPayload } from '@shared/types'
import { getDb } from '../db'
import * as db from '../db/tasks'

export function registerTaskHandlers(): void {
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.GET_ALL, (_e, opts) => db.getAllTasks(getDb(), opts))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.GET_BY_ID, (_e, { id }) => db.getTaskById(getDb(), id))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.CREATE, (_e, payload: CreateTaskPayload) =>
    db.createTask(getDb(), payload)
  )
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.UPDATE, (_e, payload: UpdateTaskPayload) =>
    db.updateTask(getDb(), payload)
  )
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.DELETE, (_e, { id }) => db.deleteTask(getDb(), id))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.REORDER, (_e, { orderedIds }) => db.reorderTasks(getDb(), orderedIds))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.COMPLETE, (_e, { id }) => db.completeTask(getDb(), id))
}

import { IPC } from '@shared/ipc-channels'
import type { CreateTaskPayload, UpdateTaskPayload } from '@shared/types'
import { getDb } from '../db'
import * as db from '../db/tasks'

export function registerTaskHandlers(): void {
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.GET_ALL, (_e, opts) => db.getAllTasks(getDb(), opts))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.GET_BY_ID, (_e, { id }) => db.getTaskById(getDb(), id))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.CREATE, (_e, payload: CreateTaskPayload) =>
    db.createTask(getDb(), payload)
  )
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.UPDATE, (_e, payload: UpdateTaskPayload) =>
    db.updateTask(getDb(), payload)
  )
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.DELETE, (_e, { id }) => db.deleteTask(getDb(), id))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.REORDER, (_e, { orderedIds }) => db.reorderTasks(getDb(), orderedIds))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.COMPLETE, (_e, { id }) => db.completeTask(getDb(), id))
}

import { ipcMain } from 'electron'
import { IPC } from '@shared/ipc-channels'
import type { CreateTaskPayload, UpdateTaskPayload } from '@shared/types'
import { getDb } from '../db'
import * as db from '../db/tasks'

export function registerTaskHandlers(): void {
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.GET_ALL, (_e, opts) => db.getAllTasks(getDb(), opts))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.GET_BY_ID, (_e, { id }) => db.getTaskById(getDb(), id))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.CREATE, (_e, payload: CreateTaskPayload) =>
    db.createTask(getDb(), payload)
  )
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.UPDATE, (_e, payload: UpdateTaskPayload) =>
    db.updateTask(getDb(), payload)
  )
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.DELETE, (_e, { id }) => db.deleteTask(getDb(), id))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.REORDER, (_e, { orderedIds }) => db.reorderTasks(getDb(), orderedIds))
  ipcMain.handle(IPC.TASKS.COMPLETE, (_e, { id }) => db.completeTask(getDb(), id))
}

// Typed wrappers around window.electron.ipcRenderer.invoke.
// The renderer imports from here — never calls ipcRenderer directly.

import { IPC } from '@shared/ipc-channels'
import type {
  Task,
  Category,
  CategoryWithCount,
  Subtask,
  CreateTaskPayload,
  UpdateTaskPayload,
  CreateCategoryPayload,
  UpdateCategoryPayload,
  CreateSubtaskPayload,
  UpdateSubtaskPayload,
  PomodoroSession
} from '@shared/types'

const invoke = (channel: string, payload?: unknown) =>
  window.electron.ipcRenderer.invoke(channel, payload)

// ── Tasks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const getAllTasks = (opts?: {
  categoryId?: number
  includeComplete?: boolean
}): Promise<Task[]> => invoke(IPC.TASKS.GET_ALL, opts)

export const getTaskById = (id: number): Promise<Task | null> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.GET_BY_ID, { id })

export const createTask = (payload: CreateTaskPayload): Promise<Task> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.CREATE, payload)

export const updateTask = (payload: UpdateTaskPayload): Promise<Task> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.UPDATE, payload)

export const deleteTask = (id: number): Promise<void> => invoke(IPC.TASKS.DELETE, { id })

export const reorderTasks = (orderedIds: number[]): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.REORDER, { orderedIds })

export const completeTask = (id: number): Promise<Task> => invoke(IPC.TASKS.COMPLETE, { id })

// ── Categories ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const getAllCategories = (): Promise<CategoryWithCount[]> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.GET_ALL)

export const createCategory = (payload: CreateCategoryPayload): Promise<Category> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.CREATE, payload)

export const updateCategory = (payload: UpdateCategoryPayload): Promise<Category> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.UPDATE, payload)

export const deleteCategory = (id: number): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.DELETE, { id })

// ── Subtasks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const getSubtasksByTask = (taskId: number): Promise<Subtask[]> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.GET_BY_TASK, { taskId })

export const createSubtask = (payload: CreateSubtaskPayload): Promise<Subtask> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.CREATE, payload)

export const updateSubtask = (payload: UpdateSubtaskPayload): Promise<Subtask> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.UPDATE, payload)

export const deleteSubtask = (id: number): Promise<void> => invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.DELETE, { id })

export const reorderSubtasks = (taskId: number, orderedIds: number[]): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.REORDER, { taskId, orderedIds })

// ── Pomodoro ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const startPomodoroSession = (payload: {
  taskId?: number
  phase: PomodoroSession['phase']
  durationS: number
}): Promise<{ sessionId: number }> => invoke(IPC.POMODORO.START_SESSION, payload)

export const completePomodoroSession = (sessionId: number): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.POMODORO.COMPLETE_SESSION, { sessionId })

// ── App ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const minimizeToTray = (): Promise<void> => invoke(IPC.APP.MINIMIZE_TO_TRAY)
export const quitApp = (): Promise<void> => invoke(IPC.APP.QUIT)

content.text:
Clean build. Everything compiles. Here's what was added:

---

## What's new

**Gamification Phase 1:**
- **XP bar** — always visible in the sidebar below the app title. Shows your level (`Lv. N`), a filling progress bar (`80/100 XP`), and today's completed Pomodoro count (🍅)
- **Task completion animation** — click the checkbox and the card flashes primary color, scales up briefly, then fades out before disappearing
- **+XP float** — a `+10 XP` label floats upward from the XP bar each time you complete a task
- All data persists across restarts in the `user_stats` SQLite table

**Auto-start on login:**
- Right-click the system tray icon → **"Start on login"** checkbox
- Toggles on/off instantly; checkmark reflects current state
- ⚠️ Note: this works correctly once the app is **packaged** (`npm run pack`). In dev mode it would register `node.exe`, not the app — so enable it after packaging

**Clickable launcher (`launch.vbs`):**
1. Open `Documents/to-dos/adhd-todo/`
2. Right-click `launch.vbs` → **"Create shortcut"**
3. Drag the shortcut to your desktop or taskbar
4. Double-click it anytime — the app opens silently, no terminal window

To launch right now: `cd Documents/to-dos/adhd-todo && npm run dev`

content.text: This is a Windows symlink permission error — electron-builder downloads code signing tools and 7zip fails to create macOS symlinks without Developer Mode enabled. The fix is to skip the installer and produce an unpacked `.exe` directly (no code signing needed):

content.content:

     6→  "scripts": {
     7→    "dev": "electron-vite dev",

     9→    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    10→    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.node.json && tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.web.json",
    11→    "rebuild": "electron-rebuild -f -w better-sqlite3",
    12→    "pack": "npm run build && electron-builder --win"
    13→  },

    15→    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.1.0",

{
  "name": "adhd-todo",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "ADHD-friendly desktop to-do app",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "electron-vite dev",
    "build": "electron-vite build",
    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.node.json && tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.web.json",
    "rebuild": "electron-rebuild -f -w better-sqlite3",
    "pack": "npm run build && electron-builder --win"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.1.0",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "^8.0.0",
    "@dnd-kit/utilities": "^3.2.2",
    "@electron-toolkit/utils": "^4.0.0",
    "@radix-ui/react-dialog": "^1.0.5",
    "@radix-ui/react-label": "^2.0.2",
    "@radix-ui/react-select": "^2.0.0",
    "@radix-ui/react-tooltip": "^1.0.7",
    "better-sqlite3": "^9.4.3",
    "class-variance-authority": "^0.7.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.0",
    "date-fns": "^3.3.1",
    "lucide-react": "^0.363.0",
    "react": "^18.2.0",
    "react-dom": "^18.2.0",
    "tailwind-merge": "^2.2.2",
    "zustand": "^4.5.2"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@electron-toolkit/tsconfig": "^1.0.1",
    "@types/better-sqlite3": "^7.6.8",
    "@types/node": "^20.11.30",
    "@types/react": "^18.2.73",
    "@types/react-dom": "^18.2.22",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.2.1",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.19",
    "electron": "^29.1.4",
    "electron-builder": "^24.13.3",
    "electron-rebuild": "^3.2.9",
    "electron-vite": "^2.0.0",
    "postcss": "^8.4.38",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.4.3",
    "typescript": "^5.4.3",
    "vite": "^5.2.6"
  },
  "build": {
    "appId": "com.user.adhd-todo",
    "productName": "ADHD Todo",
    "directories": {
      "output": "release"
    },
    "win": {
      "target": [
        {
          "target": "nsis",
          "arch": [
            "x64"
          ]
        }
      ],
      "icon": "resources/icon.ico"
    },
    "nsis": {
      "oneClick": false,
      "allowToChangeInstallationDirectory": true,
      "perMachine": false
    },
    "files": [
      "out/**",
      "resources/**"
    ],
    "asarUnpack": [
      "**/*.node"
    ]
  }
}

content.text: Also update `launch.vbs` to be smarter — launch the built exe if it exists, otherwise fall back to dev mode:

input.content:
' ADHD Todo — Silent Launcher
' Double-click this file (or a shortcut to it) to open the app without a terminal window.
' Right-click → "Create shortcut", then drag the shortcut to your desktop or taskbar.
'
' Launches the packaged exe if it exists (release/win-unpacked/),
' otherwise falls back to npm run dev (hot-reload dev mode).

Dim WshShell, fso, exePath, projectDir
Set WshShell = WScript.CreateObject("WScript.Shell")
Set fso = WScript.CreateObject("Scripting.FileSystemObject")

projectDir = "C:\Users\mbrow\Documents\to-dos\adhd-todo"
exePath = projectDir & "\release\win-unpacked\ADHD Todo.exe"

If fso.FileExists(exePath) Then
    ' Run the packaged production build
    WshShell.Run """" & exePath & """", 0, False
Else
    ' Fall back to dev mode
    WshShell.Run "cmd /c cd /d """ & projectDir & """ && npm run dev", 0, False
End If

Set WshShell = Nothing
Set fso = Nothing

content.text: electron-builder unconditionally downloads `winCodeSign` even for unsigned apps and fails because creating macOS symlinks requires Developer Mode on Windows. Let me check what's already been produced and switch to `@electron/packager` which doesn't need signing tools at all:

message.content:
Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
   - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
   - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
7. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
8. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.
9. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.
                       If there is a next step, include direct quotes from the most recent conversation showing exactly what task you were working on and where you left off. This should be verbatim to ensure there's no drift in task interpretation.

Here's an example of how your output should be structured:

<example>
<analysis>
[Your thought process, ensuring all points are covered thoroughly and accurately]
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   [Detailed description]

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - [Concept 1]
   - [Concept 2]
   - [...]

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - [File Name 1]
      - [Summary of why this file is important]
      - [Summary of the changes made to this file, if any]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [File Name 2]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [...]

4. Errors and fixes:
    - [Detailed description of error 1]:
      - [How you fixed the error]
      - [User feedback on the error if any]
    - [...]

5. Problem Solving:
   [Description of solved problems and ongoing troubleshooting]

6. All user messages: 
    - [Detailed non tool use user message]
    - [...]

7. Pending Tasks:
   - [Task 1]
   - [Task 2]
   - [...]

8. Current Work:
   [Precise description of current work]

9. Optional Next Step:
   [Optional Next step to take]

</summary>
</example>

Please provide your summary based on the conversation so far, following this structure and ensuring precision and thoroughness in your response. 

There may be additional summarization instructions provided in the included context. If so, remember to follow these instructions when creating the above summary. Examples of instructions include:
<example>
## Compact Instructions
When summarizing the conversation focus on typescript code changes and also remember the mistakes you made and how you fixed them.
</example>

<example>
# Summary instructions
When you are using compact - please focus on test output and code changes. Include file reads verbatim.
</example>


IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any tools. You MUST respond with ONLY the <summary>...</summary> block as your text output.

content.text:
<analysis>
Let me carefully analyze the entire conversation to create a comprehensive summary.

1. **Initial Request**: User wanted a desktop ADHD-friendly to-do list app with persistence, Pomodoro timer, and other ADHD-combat features.

2. **Q&A Phase**: Claude asked clarifying questions; user answered:
   - System tray + open/close window
   - Dark mode always
   - Categories visible always (ADHD: out of sight = forgotten)
   - Due dates display only + "DO BEFORE 5PM" with countdown + large clock
   - Subtasks + time estimates
   - Drag ordering + color coding
   - Recurring tasks
   - Pomodoro: 4x25/5 + 30min break, start/stop
   - Focus mode toggleable
   - Visual in-app notifications only
   - Gamification: have agent research it
   - Time estimates yes
   - Brain dump yes
   - Whatever tech stack teaches most (Electron + React + TypeScript chosen)
   - Data access optional

3. **Tech Stack Decision**: Electron + electron-vite + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui + better-sqlite3 + @dnd-kit + date-fns + Zustand + lucide-react

4. **Plan Mode**: Entered plan mode, created plan file, Plan agent designed full architecture. ExitPlanMode approved.

5. **Implementation Phase 1** (Core App):
   - Created full project structure manually (scaffolding CLI wouldn't work non-interactively)
   - All files written: package.json, electron.vite.config.ts, tsconfigs, tailwind.config.ts, postcss.config.js
   - Shared: types.ts, ipc-channels.ts
   - Main: db/migrations.ts, db/index.ts, db/categories.ts, db/tasks.ts, db/subtasks.ts, db/pomodoro.ts
   - Main IPC: tasks.ts, categories.ts, subtasks.ts, pomodoro.ts, index.ts
